title: DuckDB does not handle NULLs correctly in subquery
description:
### What happens?

DuckDB does not handle NULL values in subquery like Postgres for NOT IN operator. 

### To Reproduce

1. SETUP:

```sql
-- create outer table
CREATE TABLE outer_tab (f1 int4, f2 int4);
INSERT INTO outer_tab VALUES (0, 0);
INSERT INTO outer_tab VALUES (1, 0);

-- create inner table (one column is NULL)
CREATE TABLE inner_tab(c1 int8, c2 int8);
INSERT INTO inner_tab VALUES(0, null);
```

2. QUERY:

Postgres:

```sql
SELECT * FROM outer_tab WHERE (f1, f2) NOT IN (SELECT * FROM inner_tab);
```
```text
 f1 | f2 
----+----
  1 |  0
(1 row)
```

DuckDB:
```sql
SELECT * FROM outer_tab WHERE (f1, f2) NOT IN (SELECT * FROM inner_tab);
```
```text
┌───────┬───────┐
│  f1   │  f2   │
│ int32 │ int32 │
├───────┴───────┤
│    0 rows     │
└───────────────┘
```

Postgres compares corresponding entries as mentioned in [doc](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-subquery.html#FUNCTIONS-SUBQUERY-NOTIN:~:text=row%20is%20found.-,As%20usual%2C%20null%20values%20in%20the%20rows%20are%20combined%20per%20the,least%20one%20null%2C%20then%20the%20result%20of%20NOT%20IN%20is%20null.,-9.24.4.%C2%A0ANY).

DuckDB internally creates MARK JOIN to execute subqueries. During creation of keys, it filters the NULL values : https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/blob/main/src/execution/join_hashtable.cpp#L478, which causes the issue.

### OS:

MacOS arm64

### DuckDB Version:

v1.4.0 (Andium) b8a06e4a22

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_
